title: Resolving Incorrect Type Inference for a Generic Class

statement.inputFormat: No input is required for this problem. You only need to modify the `Container` class.
statement.outputFormat: The `getvalue` method should return the stored value of type `T`.
statement.notes: Make sure to test your solution with different types, such as `number`, `string`, and custom classes.

hints.text: Check the return type of the `getvalue` method.

editorial.title: Editorial: Resolving Type Inference for Generic Class
editorial.intuition: To fix the bug, ensure the return type of the `getvalue` method is correctly inferred by TypeScript.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve manually specifying the return type of the `getvalue` method, but this would defeat the purpose of using generics.
editorial.betterSolution: Use the `as` keyword to cast the `value` property to the correct type `T`.
editorial.optimalSolution: Modify the `getvalue` method to return the `value` property using the `as` keyword, like this: `return this.value as T;`.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected `getvalue` method ensures correct type inference for the returned value, which is essential for generic classes.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Create an instance of the `Container` class with a value of type `number`, then call the `getvalue` method to retrieve the value.
